blocks.body: Past Perfect Continuous — had been + V-ing for a process or duration that started earlier and continued up to another past action or past moment.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"Past Perfect Continuous shows a process or duration before a point in the past: the action started earlier and was still continuing before another past event or up to that moment.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the model had been + V-ing for all subjects.","example":"I had been working. / She had been waiting."},{"label":"Duration before then","color":"blue","text":"This tense helps you show how long an action continued before another past moment.","example":"They had been driving for two hours before sunset."},{"label":"Process or background","color":"amber","text":"Use it when the process or background before another past event matters.","example":"She had been studying when her friend called."},{"label":"Past cause or result","color":"violet","text":"The tense also helps explain a reason or visible result in the past with a focus on the process.","example":"He was tired because he had been working all day."}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions.","items":[{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"had been + V-ing and the main meanings of the tense."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"had not / hadn't been + V-ing for actions that were not continuing up to that point."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Had you been...?, How long had she been...? and short answers."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"for, since, how long, before, when, by the time, all day, until then."}]}

{"title":"2. Where you need this tense","sections":[{"label":"Before another past event","color":"emerald","description":"Show a process that was already continuing before another event in the past.","examples":[{"en":"They had been walking for an hour before the bus came.","ua":"The process was already in progress before the bus arrived."},{"en":"She had been cooking when the lights went out.","ua":"Cooking was the background action."}]},{"label":"Up to a past moment","color":"blue","description":"Talk about duration that led up to a specific past moment.","examples":[{"en":"By noon, I had been studying for three hours.","ua":"The duration reached that point in the past."},{"en":"We had been waiting until then.","ua":"The process continued up to that moment."}]},{"label":"Past reason or visible result","color":"amber","description":"The tense often explains why someone was in that state then.","examples":[{"en":"He was tired because he had been working all morning.","ua":"The earlier process explains the later state."},{"en":"The road was wet because it had been raining.","ua":"A past result comes from the earlier process."}]},{"label":"Process in a story","color":"violet","description":"It works well as background before another past action.","examples":[{"en":"They had been talking quietly before the teacher entered.","ua":"Talking forms the background in the story."},{"en":"I had been looking for my keys when Anna called.","ua":"The process was already happening at that time."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Past Perfect Continuous = had been + V-ing.","This tense shows an action that started earlier and continued up to another past action or past moment.","It often highlights duration, process, or a reason in the past.","Words like for, since, how long, before, by the time, and all day often point to this tense."]}
